run_summary: Portfolio up 1.9% on mark-to-market with 3 of 4 positions profitable. NTLA and PTCT thesis intact on biotech bid and own 8-K catalysts; ALGN shows deterioration on officer departure; ALK thesis weakening as Iran deal macro catalyst remains speculative. New flag pool dominated by sympathy moves and sector beta—most flagged names lack true company-specific catalysts. AYI re-entry rejected (conf 3, same thesis as prior exit). AVAV is the only high-conviction new signal (restatement + officer departure days before earnings = genuine risk to be avoided). Exploratory tier reserved for AYI-type fades if conviction were higher, and for ALGN's 5.02+7.01 pairing if context improves.

entries.name: Acuity Inc.
entries.catalyst: Earnings due 2026-06-25 pre-market; broad industrials weakness adding to the move
entries.thesis: AYI down ~7% on 1.5x volume with earnings tomorrow morning — this is positioning/de-risking ahead of the print rather than a fundamental tell. Industrials (XLI -2%) are weak but AYI's drop is roughly 3x the sector, suggesting nervous holders trimming into the catalyst. If the print is in-line or better, a sharp snap-back is likely given the magnitude of pre-positioning; if guidance is cut, today's drop will look prescient. The asymmetry tilts modestly bullish into the print because the expectation bar has been visibly lowered.
entries.what_kills: Pre-announcement tonight, channel-check leaks suggesting verticals miss, or print shows margin compression from tariff pass-through failures
entries.reasoning: Re-entry warning applies: you closed AYI 6 days ago for a +2.77% gain after entry thesis (OVERDONE conf 3, weeks horizon) deteriorated when the name continued lower instead of fading sympathy. New flag re-surfaces AYI with identical setup (OVERDONE conf 3, pre-earnings de-risking, industrials weakness) and same thesis. However, re-entry discipline requires BOTH conf >= 4 AND genuinely new information. This flag carries only conf 3 (same as exit) and the 'new information' (positioning ahead of earnings) is the SAME information cited at exit. The thesis has not changed; only the time window has advanced. Confidence must be >= 4 to override the prior loss and commit new capital. Earnings tomorrow (6/25 pre-market) is a hard catalyst, but the flag does not argue the odds have improved since the exit six days ago—it restates them. Do not re-buy at conf 3 after a recent exit on identical reasoning. WATCH for the earnings result to see if the fade thesis ultimately proved correct or if the deterioration was real.

entries.name: Onto Innovation Inc.
entries.catalyst: Broad semi-equipment selloff; CNBC flagged traders setting up cheap downside bets against chip stocks after record highs
entries.thesis: Onto down ~9% with no company-specific catalyst — empty catalyst_signals, volume exactly at average. SMH -7%, TSM -6.7%, NVDA -4%, LSCC -7%, AEIS -6% all moving same direction. ONTO as semi-equipment exposure gets dragged proportionally. The magnitude is roughly justified by the sector move; not obviously over- or underdone. Flagging as RATIONAL for audit trail — this is a beta event, not a mispricing.
entries.what_kills: ONTO-specific bad news (customer concentration disclosure, order cancellation) surfaces that would re-rate the move as fundamental rather than sympathy
entries.reasoning: Flagged RATIONAL conf 4 on pure sector sympathy (semi-equipment wreckage; SMH -7%, NVDA -4%, etc.). Volume is exactly at average (1.0x), price move is -9.2% on a day SMH is -4.8%, suggesting ONTO is 2x the sector beta but on a sector-down day with no company idiosyncratic news. The flag author's own framing ('magnitude is roughly justified by the sector move; not obviously over- or underdone') explicitly disclaims an alpha signal. Flagging RATIONAL and tagging 'beta event, not a mispricing' is a tacit concession that this is noise. Conf 4 on pure sympathy does not justify a position in a portfolio that already has healthcare beta (NTLA, PTCT, ALGN). No company-specific catalyst, no pricing asymmetry, no learning signal. Skip.

entries.name: Calix, Inc
entries.catalyst: Unknown — elevated volume without visible news
entries.thesis: CALX -3% today on 2.66x average volume — the volume spike is the interesting signal, not the price move. No 8-K, no scheduled earnings, no obvious peer drag (broadband-access equipment isn't part of the SMH wreckage). High-volume selling in a $2.3B name without identifiable catalyst often reflects fund-level repositioning that reverses when the seller is done. Tech-sector beta weak today provides cover but doesn't fully explain. Modest fade setup if next session shows volume normalizing.
entries.what_kills: BEAD/rural broadband funding cut announcement or major tier-1 telco capex revision — this would justify and extend the move
entries.reasoning: OVERDONE conf 3 on a small-cap (-2.96% move on 2.66x volume) with no identified catalyst. Flag reasoning is sound: elevated volume without news often reflects single-fund positioning unwind that reverses. However, the volume spike occurred today and the thesis requires waiting 1-2 sessions to see if volume normalizes (the confirm condition). No catalyst URL, no SEC filing, pure momentum signal. The setup is plausible for a fade, but entering before the confirm condition (volume normalization) is premature. The risk/reward is better WATCHED for a bounce attempt on lower volume, which would tighten entry logic and reduce downside surprise. Hold for better entry shape.

entries.name: AeroVironment, Inc.
entries.catalyst: 8-K filed 2026-06-22 with Item 4.02 (restatement) and 5.02 (officer change); earnings scheduled 6/29 after-hours
entries.thesis: Item 4.02 — non-reliance on previously issued financials — is the single most severe 8-K item code, and pairing it with a 5.02 officer departure days before a scheduled earnings print is textbook bad-news bundling. A ~11% one-day drop on a defense name with this filing combo is, if anything, restrained — investors who think they're buying the dip may be underestimating that prior financials are no longer trustworthy and earnings in 7 days are now an open question (possible delay, possible going-concern language, possible auditor change).
entries.what_kills: Restatement turns out to be narrow/technical (e.g., reclassification with no income statement impact) and the 5.02 departure is a planned retirement with successor named — in which case the drop is the overreaction and snaps back
entries.reasoning: Flagged RATIONAL conf 4 on Item 4.02 restatement + 5.02 officer departure filed 2026-06-22, with earnings due 6/29 (7 days out). The flag author's reasoning is explicit: Item 4.02 (non-reliance on prior financials) is the most severe 8-K item; the pairing with officer departure suggests pending disclosure of restatement magnitude and possible auditor change or going-concern language. The flag correctly identifies that an ~11% one-day drop is "restrained" and that fading the first-day move is a losing trade historically (second leg down comes when magnitude is quantified). This is NOT a mispricing to exploit—it is a deteriorating fundamental risk that should be avoided. The position would likely be wrong, not profitable. Skip (avoid the name entirely, do not short or fade).

entries.name: Align Technology, Inc.
entries.catalyst: 8-K filed 2026-06-22 with Items 5.02 (officer change) and 7.01 (Reg FD)
entries.thesis: ALGN dropping ~4.5% on a 5.02 + 7.01 combo while XLV is up 0.44% and XBI up 3.65% suggests the market is reading the departure as material. But 5.02 without an accompanying 2.02 (earnings) or 4.02 (restatement) is usually a single-event story — and ALGN is a quality compounder that has historically been a fade-the-overreaction name. If this is a planned CEO/CFO succession with named successor (which the parallel 7.01 Reg FD often signals — companies pair them when they want to control narrative), the drop is likely overdone in a tape with healthcare bid.
entries.what_kills: Departure is the CFO mid-quarter with no successor named, OR the Reg FD disclosure contains a guidance cut — either makes the drop justified or even insufficient
entries.reasoning: New flag on ALGN as OVERDONE conf 3 (same name as open position, different catalyst angle). Open position thesis was weakening (see position decision above). New flag cites Items 5.02 (officer change) + 7.01 (Reg FD) filed 6/22, and argues that pairing these items often signals a managed succession announcement, making the -4.57% drop overdone IF the Reg FD contains no guidance cut. This is a coherent fade thesis in a strong XLV (+0.44%) / XBI (+3.65%) tape. However, execution risk is high: the flag is correct that the 5.02/7.01 pairing pattern can signal managed succession, but WITHOUT reading the exhibit I cannot confirm that is the case here. Additionally, the open position is already on book at -4.8%, and initiating a second exploratory position in the same name creates concentration risk (would breach sector limits if we are not careful). Better action: stay with the WATCH recommendation in the position decision above, read the 8-K exhibit to confirm successor/guidance details, then decide on both a position add and a new flag fade. Do not add now.

entries.name: Kratos Defense & Security Solutions
entries.catalyst: No company-specific catalyst; appears to be sympathy with AVAV restatement news
entries.thesis: KTOS down ~5.8% with empty catalyst_signals on a day AVAV (the most comparable small-drone-defense peer) cratered on company-specific restatement news. ITA down only 1.46% — KTOS is selling at ~4x the sector move on what appears to be AVAV contagion. The accounting issue at AVAV is not a sector-wide read-across; investors conflating them creates an asymmetric fade setup once AVAV's filing is properly digested.
entries.what_kills: KTOS files its own negative 8-K; defense budget headlines turn negative; AVAV restatement reveals an industry-wide accounting practice issue (unlikely but possible)
entries.reasoning: OVERDONE conf 3 flagged as pure sympathy with AVAV restatement (-5.76% on 0.98x volume, empty catalyst signals). The flag's own reasoning: AVAV is the comparable peer, KTOS selling at ~4x the sector move (ITA only -1.46%), and the accounting issue at AVAV is NOT a KTOS-specific risk. This is correctly identified as a contagion fade candidate. However, the setup requires that AVAV's filing is 'properly digested' (flag's own confirm condition), which takes time. Entering KTOS now on the bet that it will fade sympathy is a directional call on sentiment, not a fundamental signal. The flag does not argue KTOS has new positive news or an earnings catalyst; it only argues the sympathy is transient. Wait for either (a) a 1-2 session stabilization in KTOS independent of AVAV news flow, or (b) KTOS-specific positive news. Skip now, WATCH for entry on condition.

entries.name: JetBlue Airways Corporation
entries.catalyst: Strait of Hormuz oil tanker traffic resumption pushing oil lower; JBLU as highest fuel-sensitivity carrier
entries.thesis: Double-digit move on a small-cap airline with no 8-K likely tracks the Strait of Hormuz reopening and the resulting oil weakness (XLE -1.65%) — JBLU is the highest-beta US carrier on jet fuel, so it amplifies any oil-down narrative. But moves of this magnitude on macro alone, in a structurally challenged carrier with weak balance sheet, tend to fade once the energy narrative stabilizes. Bear-side bias on fade.
entries.what_kills: An actual JBLU-specific catalyst emerges (merger talk, route deal, guidance raise) — would reframe as fundamental rather than macro sympathy
entries.reasoning: OVERDONE conf 3 on +10.72% daily move and +14.06% five-day move, flagged as macro-driven (Strait of Hormuz re-opening → oil weakness → airline fuel relief tailwind). Flag reasoning is sound: double-digit move on macro alone, in a structurally weak carrier, tends to fade when the energy narrative stabilizes. However, the setup is pure momentum fade with no company catalyst—entry timing is speculative, and the flag's own note about 'bear-side bias on fade' suggests uncertainty. The five-day move of +14% is large but not extreme for a high-beta, high-short-interest carrier in a risk-on bounce. No SEC filing, no earnings catalyst, no pricing asymmetry beyond macro sentiment. SKIP—wait for oil to stabilize and JBLU volume to collapse (showing the fade is in motion) before entering.

entries.name: Advanced Energy Industries
entries.catalyst: Semi rally; possible residual effect of 6/12 8-K
entries.thesis: AEIS is power conversion for semi-cap equipment — direct beneficiary of the chip rally. The 8-K from June 12 had only 8.01 + 9.01 items (catch-all + boilerplate), which are not severity indicators on their own. Move appears to be sector beta, not idiosyncratic.
entries.what_kills: If the 8.01 turned out to be a guidance pre-announcement (we can't read the URL contents from here), the move could be idiosyncratic and have more legs
entries.reasoning: Flagged RATIONAL conf 3 on +5.45% daily move and +9.46% five-day move, identified as semi-equipment sector beta (power-conversion exposure to chip-cap rally). Flag's own reasoning: move appears to be sector beta, not idiosyncratic. June 12 8-K contained only 8.01 (other events) + 9.01 (boilerplate) with no severity indicators. The flag author notes they cannot read the 8-K exhibit and cannot determine if the 8.01 was guidance-material. This is insufficient clarity for entry—if the move is pure SMH beta, there is no alpha. If the 8-K was material, the flag should be clearer about it. SKIP pending either (a) a read of the 8-K exhibit to identify the 8.01 content, or (b) confirmation that the move tracks SMH exactly (which would make it a sector hedge, not a standalone position).

entries.name: Intellia Therapeutics, Inc.
entries.catalyst: UniQure announcing FDA filing for Huntington's gene therapy lifting gene-editing group; NTLA Reg FD 8-K on 6/15
entries.thesis: NTLA up modestly today but a notable multi-day run (extended five-day gain) in sympathy with UniQure's announcement that it will seek FDA approval for its Huntington's gene therapy. Combined with NTLA's own 6/15 8-K (Reg FD + 8.01), this looks like a coherent, justified re-rating of gene-edit/gene-therapy regulatory optionality rather than an overreaction. Risk-off tape did not prevent the move — XBI +3% confirms group bid.
entries.what_kills: NTLA 8-K turns out to be a routine conference presentation deck with no new data; UniQure FDA process stalls or pushback from agency reappears; XBI rolls over with broader risk-off resumption.
entries.reasoning: New flag on NTLA as RATIONAL conf 3, citing UniQure FDA-pathway news lifting the gene-edit cohort and NTLA's own 6/15 8-K (Reg FD + 8.01). Position is already open and up +19.1% on 7 days held; thesis is intact per position decision above. New flag confirms the thesis (group re-rating, own 8-K catalyst) but offers no new entry signal—name is already long. SKIP as a new position (already owned at better prices).

entries.name: PTC Therapeutics, Inc.
entries.catalyst: PTCT 8-K (8.01) filed 6/16 plus broader biotech bid
entries.thesis: PTCT up sharply on roughly 2x volume with an 8-K filed yesterday (items 8.01+9.01, i.e., 'other event' with exhibits — typically a press release for a regulatory update, partnership, or pipeline news). XBI +3% provides a supportive tape. Move appears appropriate to a non-trivial disclosure rather than excessive; without reading the exhibit I can't argue it's underdone, but the combination of own filing + group strength makes the magnitude defensible.
entries.what_kills: Exhibit is routine corporate update with no new value-driving content — would suggest the move overshot and is faded once read
entries.reasoning: New flag on PTCT as RATIONAL conf 3, citing 8-K filed 6/16 (8.01 + 9.01 items) + XBI group strength. Position is already open and up +8.52% on 5 days held; thesis is intact per position decision above. Flag confirms the thesis but offers no new entry—name is already held. SKIP.

entries.name: Veeco Instruments Inc.
entries.catalyst: No company-specific catalyst; sympathy with SMH -4.8% and broader semi sell-off (AVGO, TSM, NVDA all down hard)
entries.thesis: VECO ran up notably over the past week alongside the semi-equipment cohort (ONTO, AEIS up similar amounts) and is now unwinding harder than the SMH index because of low float and momentum positioning. No 8-K, no earnings catalyst — this is sector beta plus crowding unwind, and an outsized one-day drop in a name that just rallied tends to overshoot fundamentals.
entries.what_kills: If a delayed news source surfaces a VECO-specific negative (customer push-out, downgrade with channel checks), the move is fundamentally driven and not a fade.
entries.reasoning: OVERDONE conf 3 on -9.44% daily drop after a +11.92% five-day rally (VECO rallied with semi-equipment peers ONTO, AEIS, then unwound harder on no company-specific catalyst). Flag reasoning: low-float crowding unwind on a sector-down day (SMH -4.8%), not fundamentals. Classic fade setup IF the sector base holds and SMH stabilizes. However, the confirm condition requires either SMH stabilization tomorrow OR a peer/customer pre-announcement of caution (LRCX, AMAT). The risk is that semi-cap weakness extends further, dragging VECO lower in a negative feedback loop. WATCH for SMH stabilization and VECO volume patterns over next session; if volume normalizes on a bounce, re-flag for exploratory entry. Do not front-run the fade without seeing the sector stabilization first.

entries.name: Alpha Metallurgical Resources
entries.catalyst: 6/18 Reg FD 8-K of unclear content; ongoing met-coal commodity weakness
entries.thesis: AMR down nearly 5% today and ~12% over 5 days, with a 7.01 Reg FD disclosure filed last week. Reg FD items are catch-all — could be investor presentation, could be guidance update — and without the filing contents we cannot tell which. Coking coal pricing is also pressured by global steel demand uncertainty. Cannot construct a 3+ confidence call in either direction without reading the 6/18 8-K exhibit.
entries.what_kills: Filing turns out to be benign (conference presentation) — then today's selling is pure commodity tape and likely RATIONAL given met-coal spot weakness
entries.reasoning: UNCLEAR classification — no directional edge.

entries.name: Beam Therapeutics
entries.catalyst: Sector rotation into biotech; possible takeout/data speculation not in 8-K stream
entries.thesis: BEAM up on the day in line with a very strong XBI tape, extending a notable multi-day run. No catalyst_signals means the move is riding the sector bid rather than a discrete event. Magnitude is reasonable given XBI's strength; nothing to fade or chase without identifying the specific driver of the multi-day run.
entries.what_kills: XBI rolls over; BEAM gives back gains faster than the sector
entries.reasoning: RATIONAL classification — no directional edge.

entries.name: Pool Corporation
entries.catalyst: Unknown — no 8-K, no news, no earnings proximity flagged
entries.thesis: POOL trading at nearly 7x average volume on a modest price move is unusual — volume disconnect from price typically signals institutional repositioning, index rebalance, or a block trade rather than fundamental news. No 8-K, no earnings window flagged. Cannot articulate directional thesis without knowing the source of flow.
entries.what_kills: Volume turns out to be a single block cross with no follow-through tomorrow
entries.reasoning: UNCLEAR classification — no directional edge.

entries.name: Watsco, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Apparent building-products sector bid; no 8-K
entries.thesis: WSO up alongside BLDR with no company news suggests sympathy buying on a building products day. Magnitude is in line with the risk-on tape and small/mid industrial bid (XLI +0.73%, IWM +2%). No mispricing call — just observe that the housing-adjacent distributors are trading together.
entries.what_kills: WSO-specific 8-K appears showing M&A or guide cut/raise
entries.reasoning: RATIONAL classification — no directional edge.

entries.name: Exelixis, Inc.
entries.catalyst: USTR tariff probe on Germany drug pricing dragging pharma sector
entries.thesis: EXEL trading heavy on elevated volume against an up tape, in line with XLV -0.87% weakness. The US tariff probe on German drug pricing is a pharma policy headline that drags pharma broadly; EXEL is US-focused oncology so the read-through is indirect. Slight overdone bias — pricing policy fears tend to mean-revert in single-drug oncology names without direct EU exposure — but conviction low without firm-specific clarity.
entries.what_kills: An EXEL-specific negative readout, FDA action, or competitive trial result emerges — would justify the weakness fundamentally
entries.reasoning: confidence 2 below buy threshold.

entries.catalyst: Unknown — no 8-K, no earnings; possible LTL tonnage data, downgrade, or peer preannouncement
entries.thesis: Sharp multi-day decline on no visible catalyst — no 8-K, no scheduled earnings. XLI is roughly flat (-0.14%), so this is not sector tape. Could be a monthly LTL tonnage update, competitor preannouncement, or analyst downgrade not captured in the news feed. Cannot construct a directional thesis at conf 3+ without identifying the driver.
entries.what_kills: n/a — need to identify the catalyst first
entries.reasoning: UNCLEAR classification — no directional edge.

entries.catalyst: Unknown — no 8-K, no earnings catalyst visible
entries.thesis: Multi-day decline in a software name with no 8-K and no scheduled earnings. XLK is only down 0.34% so this is not pure tech tape. Could be sell-side derating on supply-chain software demand concerns, a competitor read-through, or a slow-bleed sentiment shift; without an identifiable catalyst I cannot commit to overdone/underdone at conf 3+.
entries.what_kills: n/a
entries.reasoning: UNCLEAR classification — no directional edge.

entries.catalyst: Sector risk-off in consumer discretionary on hawkish Fed re-statement
entries.thesis: DECK down on below-average volume on a day XLY is -2.5% and the broader risk-off impulse from a more hawkish Fed is hitting discretionary hardest. No company-specific catalyst, no 8-K, no earnings. Magnitude is roughly consistent with sector beta plus mid-cap discretionary skew — nothing to fade or chase.
entries.what_kills: DECK accelerates lower on rising volume tomorrow without sector follow-through, suggesting an undisclosed name-specific issue
entries.reasoning: RATIONAL classification — no directional edge.

entries.name: Qorvo, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Sympathy with semi/Apple-supplier weakness; possible digestion of June 12 material agreement
entries.thesis: QRVO is moving roughly in line with the broader semi/Apple-supply complex on a clear sector-down day, but the June 12 1.01 filing (material definitive agreement) is non-trivial — could be M&A, a big customer contract, a debt covenant amendment, or a divestiture. Without reading the exhibit, the direction the 1.01 should push the stock is unknowable, and today's move is consistent with pure semi-tape sympathy regardless.
entries.what_kills: Without the exhibit, neither side rises above conf 3.
entries.reasoning: UNCLEAR classification — no directional edge.
